explanation: The key requirements in start-up mode are: (1) The controller initializes itself with default configuration parameters if any (represented as 'initStart' leads to 'defaultParamsLoaded'), (2) checks the system memory (implied by progressing to next steps), (3) checks communication with sensors ('checkCommsSensors'), valves ('checkCommsValves'), and GUI ('checkCommsGUI'), and (4) start-up mode only terminates once all these activities are completed (represented by 'initDone'). Thus, whenever the system is in 'StartUpMode', eventually (F) all these steps must happen before 'initDone' can be true. This can be formalized as: 'In every state, if StartUpMode, then eventually (F) defaultParamsLoaded, checkCommsSensors, checkCommsValves, checkCommsGUI, and finally initDone.' In LTL, we use implication and F (finally/eventually) as follows: G(StartUpMode -> (F defaultParamsLoaded & F checkCommsSensors & F checkCommsValves & F checkCommsGUI & F initDone)).

explanation_dict.natural_language: Initialize with default parameters if any
Start-up mode is only completed when all activities are completed; represents successful completion
In every state, if in StartUpMode, then eventually these activities must happen
explanation_dict.LTL: G(StartUpMode -> (F defaultParamsLoaded & F checkCommsSensors & F checkCommsValves & F checkCommsGUI & F initDone))

output_LTL: G(StartUpMode -> (F defaultParamsLoaded & F checkCommsSensors & F checkCommsValves & F checkCommsGUI & F initDone))

explanation:
To formalize the Start-up Mode, we need to represent that whenever the system is in StartUpMode, the controller must (1) load default configuration parameters (if any), (2) check the system memory and communication with sensors, valves, and GUI, (3) only proceed to complete the start-up after all these activities. Let:
- StartUpMode: system is in start-up mode
- defaultParamsLoaded: default params loaded
- checkCommsSensors: checked communication with sensors
- checkCommsValves: checked communication with valves
- checkCommsGUI: checked communication with GUI
- initDone: signifies configuration loaded, internal checking terminated (i.e., start-up complete)

The globally (G) operator is used because the requirements must hold at every state in StartUpMode. For the main requirement: When in StartUpMode, it is required that (1) default parameters are (if any) loaded, and (2) all communications have been checked before initDone can become true (before start-up can complete).

To force that initDone cannot occur before the required steps, we express:
- Globally, if in StartUpMode and initDone occurs, then prior to initDone, defaultParamsLoaded and checkCommsSensors and checkCommsValves and checkCommsGUI have all occurred.

In LTL, to enforce that something must have happened before another event, we can use implication inside an until: While in StartUpMode, until initDone, all the checks and loading must occur before initDone. However, since LTL is inherently future-oriented, it's common to express that 'initDone' can only occur once all the preconditions are met, i.e., that until all are satisfied, initDone cannot happen.

Thus, a direct formula:
G ( (StartUpMode & ! (defaultParamsLoaded & checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves & checkCommsGUI)) -> !initDone)

And, perhaps: G(StartUpMode & initDone -> (defaultParamsLoaded & checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves & checkCommsGUI))

That is, whenever start-up mode completes (initDone), all steps must have occurred.

We present both for clarity:
1. G(StartUpMode & initDone -> (defaultParamsLoaded & checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves & checkCommsGUI))
2. G(StartUpMode & ! (defaultParamsLoaded & checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves & checkCommsGUI) -> !initDone)

But formula 1 is the typical way for LTL specifications for "cannot complete until previous tasks are finished."

explanation_dict.natural_language: InitDone can only occur if all checks and default loading have been done
Globally, if we are in StartUpMode and initDone, then all required activities happened before

explanation: We are asked to translate requirements for 'Start-up Mode', particularly focusing on the initialization, checking memory, checking communication with sensors, valves, and GUI, and that start-up completes once all required activities finish. We map each described activity to an atomic proposition. The sequence is: (1) system enters StartUpMode; (2) defaultParamsLoaded (if any); (3) checkCommsSensors, checkCommsValves, checkCommsGUI, memory check (not explicitly present, so can be omitted or abstracted); (4) complete all checks; (5) exit StartUpMode (i.e., initDone). The core property is: StartUpMode holds until initDone, with required activities occurring before initDone. This can be expressed as: If StartUpMode holds, then until initDone: any parameters that must be loaded (defaultParamsLoaded), and checkCommsSensors, checkCommsValves, checkCommsGUI all eventually complete (we express this with implication and 'eventually F'). Also, StartUpMode should not end before initDone. The formalization becomes G(StartUpMode -> ((!initDone) U (initDone & F defaultParamsLoaded & F checkCommsSensors & F checkCommsValves & F checkCommsGUI))). If defaultParamsLoaded is not always required, we can make F defaultParamsLoaded optional.

explanation_dict.natural_language: Start-up mode should not complete until all required activities completed
explanation_dict.LTL: StartUpMode U (initDone & F checkCommsSensors & F checkCommsValves & F checkCommsGUI)

explanation_dict.natural_language: if there are default configuration parameters, they are loaded (optionally)
All communications (sensors, valves, GUI) are checked during start-up
explanation_dict.LTL: F checkCommsSensors & F checkCommsValves & F checkCommsGUI

explanation_dict.natural_language: Globally, if we are in StartUpMode, then eventually (possibly after many steps) all these checks are done before initDone
explanation_dict.LTL: G(StartUpMode -> ( (!initDone) U (initDone & F defaultParamsLoaded & F checkCommsSensors & F checkCommsValves & F checkCommsGUI) ))

output_LTL: G(StartUpMode -> ( (!initDone) U (initDone & F defaultParamsLoaded & F checkCommsSensors & F checkCommsValves & F checkCommsGUI) ))

explanation:
This requirement describes the Start-up Mode, during which the controller initializes itself with default parameters (if any), then checks the system memory and communication with sensors/valves and the GUI. The mode is completed once all these activities are done. To capture this as an LTL formula, we first break down the activities: 1) Load defaults (defaultParamsLoaded), 2) Perform memory checks and communication checks with sensors (checkCommsSensors), with valves (checkCommsValves), and with the GUI (checkCommsGUI). The Start-Up Mode (StartUpMode) is active during this process. When all initialization and checks are done (initDone), StartUpMode is completed (becomes inactive). So, while StartUpMode is active, the system must eventually complete each required step (defaults, comms, etc.), and must stay in StartUpMode until initialization is completed (initDone). This can be expressed with two formulas: (1) When StartUpMode becomes active, it must remain active until initialization is done (StartUpMode -> (¬initDone U initDone)), and (2) While StartUpMode is active, eventually each subactivity happens (G(StartUpMode -> F(defaultParamsLoaded))), (similar for checks).

Putting these components together, the main formula can be:
G(StartUpMode -> ((¬initDone) U initDone)) 
and 
G(StartUpMode -> (F(defaultParamsLoaded) & F(checkCommsSensors) & F(checkCommsValves) & F(checkCommsGUI)))

The first formula means: Whenever StartUpMode holds, it must continue until initDone holds. The second formula means: Whenever StartUpMode holds, all required steps will eventually complete. These can be joined with AND (&) for the complete property.

explanation_dict.natural_language: While in StartUpMode, proceed until initDone and eventually complete each required activity
explanation_dict.LTL: G(StartUpMode -> ((¬initDone) U initDone) & F(defaultParamsLoaded) & F(checkCommsSensors) & F(checkCommsValves) & F(checkCommsGUI))

output_LTL: G(StartUpMode -> (((!initDone) U initDone) & F(defaultParamsLoaded) & F(checkCommsSensors) & F(checkCommsValves) & F(checkCommsGUI)))

explanation:
The requirements describe the behavior of the system during Start-up Mode. In Start-up Mode, the controller must (1) load default configuration parameters, (2) check system memory, (3) check communication with sensors and valves and the GUI, and (4) only proceed to completion once all activities are done. We are to capture, in LTL, that whenever the system is in StartUpMode, these checks and initializations eventually happen, and that StartUpMode is not exited until all these are done. We choose atomic propositions as follows: StartUpMode represents that the controller is in start-up mode. defaultParamsLoaded indicates default parameters initialization, checkCommsSensors, checkCommsValves, and checkCommsGUI indicate communications checks, and initDone represents that all required activities are completed.      

To capture these requirements we need two properties:

1. Whenever we are in StartUpMode, all required initialization checks will eventually be completed (eventually initDone and the required setup/checks are done).
2. The StartUpMode is only considered complete (i.e., can be exited) once all initialization has been done.

We can capture property 1 as: G(StartUpMode -> (F defaultParamsLoaded & F checkCommsSensors & F checkCommsValves & F checkCommsGUI & F initDone))

(Meaning: always, if you are in start-up, eventually default params are loaded, communication checks with sensors/valves/GUI are completed, and initDone.)

We can capture property 2 as: (StartUpMode & !(defaultParamsLoaded & checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves & checkCommsGUI & initDone)) -> X StartUpMode

(Meaning: if we are in StartUpMode and not all checks/initialization done, we stay in StartUpMode at the next step.)

Together, these LTL formulas formalize the natural language statement.

explanation_dict.natural_language: In Start-up Mode, the controller initializes itself with default configuration parameters.
the controller checks communication with sensors, valves, and GUI
explanation_dict.LTL: StartUpMode -> F checkCommsSensors & F checkCommsValves & F checkCommsGUI

explanation_dict.natural_language: Start-up mode is completed once the required activities have been completed
explanation_dict.LTL: (StartUpMode & !(defaultParamsLoaded & checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves & checkCommsGUI & initDone)) -> X StartUpMode

output_LTL: G(StartUpMode -> (F defaultParamsLoaded & F checkCommsSensors & F checkCommsValves & F checkCommsGUI & F initDone)) & G((StartUpMode & !(defaultParamsLoaded & checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves & checkCommsGUI & initDone)) -> X StartUpMode)

explanation:
The start-up mode requires sequential completion of a set of initialization and system check activities, after which start-up mode is considered done. The main activities mentioned are: (1) controller initializes itself with default configuration parameters (i.e., 'defaultParamsLoaded'), (2) checks the system memory (internal/implicit in 'initDone'), and (3) checks communication with sensors and valves (represented by 'checkCommsSensors', 'checkCommsValves'), as well as with the GUI (represented by 'checkCommsGUI'). The start-up completes when all required initialization/check activities are done (represented here as 'initDone'). 

To translate, we interpret this as: 
- If StartUpMode is active, then eventually all the required steps (default parameter loading, comms with sensors/valves/GUI checking, etc.) must happen, and finally when all is finished, initDone becomes true and StartUpMode can end. 
- Typically, in the system, these actions occur after initialization is started ('initStart'), so 'initStart' triggers this sequence.
- We can write this as: Globally, if StartUpMode is true, then eventually defaultParamsLoaded, checkCommsSensors, checkCommsValves, and checkCommsGUI will all be true, and eventually initDone will be true, and StartUpMode will eventually end thereafter.

We decompose the explanation:
1. When in StartUpMode, eventually defaultParamsLoaded
2. When in StartUpMode, eventually checkCommsSensors
3. When in StartUpMode, eventually checkCommsValves
4. When in StartUpMode, eventually checkCommsGUI
5. When in StartUpMode, eventually initDone

Assuming these steps can occur in any order or possibly in parallel (unless specified otherwise), we conjunct the F (eventually) obligations together, all gated by StartUpMode.

The overall system safety property is: while in StartUpMode, the required initialization activities must eventually occur.

explanation_dict.LTL: G(StartUpMode -> (F defaultParamsLoaded & F checkCommsSensors & F checkCommsValves & F checkCommsGUI & F initDone))

output_LTL: G(StartUpMode -> (F defaultParamsLoaded & F checkCommsSensors & F checkCommsValves & F checkCommsGUI & F initDone))

explanation: The Start-up Mode consists of several steps that must be completed before exiting the mode. According to the natural language, the steps are: (1) initialization begins (initStart), (2) default parameters (if any) are loaded (defaultParamsLoaded), (3) check communication with sensors (checkCommsSensors), (4) check communication with valves (checkCommsValves), (5) check communication with GUI (checkCommsGUI), and (6) initialization is done (initDone). The mode should not be completed (StartUpMode ends) until all required activities are done. In LTL, this means that while in StartUpMode, the completion (initDone) cannot occur before all the activities have been completed after initialization starts. The usual way to specify such a progress property is: Whenever we are in StartUpMode, then initDone (the completion of startup) happens only after all requirements above are checked since the start. Using auxiliary progress subformulas for clarity: 1. 'Initialization starts' (initStart). 2. 'Default parameters loaded' (defaultParamsLoaded), etc. 3. 'Startup mode completes only if all steps have happened' translates to: Whenever initDone happens, then in the past since the most recent initStart, all other required activities occurred. In forward LTL using 'G' and '->': Globally, if in StartUpMode and initDone occurs, then defaultParamsLoaded, checkCommsSensors, checkCommsValves, and checkCommsGUI have all happened previously since the last initStart. In pure future LTL, we approximate this as: It is always the case (globally, G) that if we are in StartUpMode and 'initDone' occurs, then in the past, after 'initStart', all required activities have occurred. Since LTL cannot express 'since' or 'previously' directly, we approximate it by requiring: After each initStart, all the required actions must eventually occur before initDone can occur. Furthermore, before initDone happens, if StartUpMode is active, all activities must have been completed. One way is: start-up can only finish (initDone) after the other actions finish. We express this as: Globally, if StartUpMode and initDone, then defaultParamsLoaded and checkCommsSensors and checkCommsValves and checkCommsGUI have all occurred previously in this episode of StartUpMode. Given LTL limitations, we state: Globally, if in StartUpMode, then (if initDone, then all necessary actions have completed): G(StartUpMode -> (initDone -> (defaultParamsLoaded & checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves & checkCommsGUI))) This formula means: At any time during StartUpMode, if initDone occurs, it must be the case that all key activities have also occurred. (Further liveness, like 'eventually initDone' per 'StartUpMode' episode, could be added for completeness.)

explanation_dict.natural_language: Check/test communication of the controller with sensors
Check/test communication of the controller with valves
Check/test communication between the controller and GUI
If in StartUpMode and initDone, then all steps must have happened

explanation: The Start-up Mode involves a sequence of initialization steps that must all be successfully completed before the Start-up Mode is considered completed. These steps are: (1) Loading default configuration parameters if any, (2) checking system memory, (3) checking communication with sensors and valves, (4) checking communication with the GUI. We assume that the atomic propositions that reflect these checks and initialization are: initStart (initialization starts), defaultParamsLoaded (defaults loaded), checkCommsSensors, checkCommsValves, checkCommsGUI (communication checks to sensors, valves, GUI), and initDone (all tasks completed). We also include that StartUpMode is active during all these activities, and that StartUpMode ends only after initDone. The key specification is that once in StartUpMode (StartUpMode), eventually all required steps occur (F(defaultParamsLoaded & checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves & checkCommsGUI & initDone)). Also, StartUpMode should only be completed after (never before) all steps completed, i.e., StartUpMode must hold until initDone, expressed as StartUpMode U initDone. In summary: (1) If StartUpMode becomes true, eventually all initialization sub-steps must happen and (2) StartUpMode holds at least until initDone. We can represent the requirements as: (G(StartUpMode -> F(defaultParamsLoaded & checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves & checkCommsGUI & initDone)) & (StartUpMode -> (StartUpMode U initDone))).

explanation_dict.natural_language: In start-up mode, all initialization steps must eventually occur: load default params, check comms with sensors, valves, GUI, and complete internal checking.
explanation_dict.LTL: G(StartUpMode -> F(defaultParamsLoaded & checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves & checkCommsGUI & initDone))

explanation_dict.natural_language: Start-up mode is active until all start-up steps are completed, i.e., until initDone becomes true.

output_LTL: (G(StartUpMode -> F(defaultParamsLoaded & checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves & checkCommsGUI & initDone)) & (StartUpMode -> (StartUpMode U initDone)))

explanation: The Start-up Mode requires that, once initialization starts (initStart), the controller processes several actions: loads its default parameters if there are any (defaultParamsLoaded), checks the communications with sensors (checkCommsSensors), valves (checkCommsValves), and GUI (checkCommsGUI), and only once all these have been completed, initDone becomes true and Start-up Mode is completed. We capture that after initStart, all of these sub-tasks occur (possibly in any order, but all must finish before initDone), and Start-up Mode continues until initDone. We translate 'initializes itself with default configuration parameters (if any)' as eventually defaultParamsLoaded after initStart, communication checks as eventual check* after initStart, and Start-up as a mode (StartUpMode). We want that while StartUpMode is active, before initDone, all of these must eventually be done, and once initDone holds, StartUpMode can be finished. For simplicity, we specify: whenever StartUpMode is active, then eventually (while still in StartUpMode, i.e., until initDone) each of defaultParamsLoaded, checkCommsSensors, checkCommsValves, and checkCommsGUI must be true. The completion is signaled by initDone. This can be captured in LTL as: G(StartUpMode -> ((!(initDone)) -> (F (defaultParamsLoaded & checkCommsSensors & checkCommsValves & checkCommsGUI & initDone)))).

explanation_dict.natural_language: StartUpMode is active (system is in start-up mode)
Controller loads default configuration parameters (if any)
While in StartUpMode and before initDone, all sub-tasks (default param load, comm checks) occur eventually before exit
Globally, whenever StartUpMode is active, require this

explanation: The start-up mode requirements are: (1) The controller initializes itself with default configuration parameters, (2) It checks system memory, (3) It checks communication with sensors, valves, and GUI, (4) Start-up mode ends once all required activities have been completed. We encode these as follows: initStart triggers loading defaultParamsLoaded, checkCommsSensors, checkCommsValves, and checkCommsGUI, with all these verifications completed before exiting StartUpMode. Thus, globally, whenever StartUpMode starts (initStart), it is followed by defaultParamsLoaded, checkCommsSensors, checkCommsValves, and checkCommsGUI, and once all are done, a signal (initDone) indicates completion of start-up mode, after which StartUpMode no longer holds. For guard, failures such as initFail or hardware errors are not requested by the problem statement, so we omit them. The central part: 'Start-up mode is completed once the required activities have been completed' is formalized so that once initDone is true, StartUpMode should eventually stop holding. The LTL formula thus encodes that during StartUpMode the controller must finish the four steps, and when done, StartUpMode is exited.

explanation_dict.natural_language: Controller initializes itself with default configuration parameters (if any).
Checks system memory, controller communication with sensors, valves, and GUI.
Start-up mode is completed once required activities are completed (initDone). Once initDone holds, StartUpMode is eventually deasserted.

output_LTL: G((StartUpMode & initStart) -> (F defaultParamsLoaded & F checkCommsSensors & F checkCommsValves & F checkCommsGUI)) & G((StartUpMode & initDone) -> F (!StartUpMode))
